# 2FA Authenticator

> 2FA Authenticator is a pure static, browser-only TOTP generator. Zero third-party network requests, all secret handling happens locally in your browser.

## What it does
- Generate 6-digit TOTP codes (RFC 6238, HMAC-SHA1, 30s period) in the browser
- Accept Base32 secret input or scan otpauth:// QR codes
- Save multiple accounts in localStorage with a Cookie backup
- Installable as a PWA, works fully offline after first load
- Single-file source, auditable

## Public pages
- Homepage (tool + FAQ + How it works + Compatibility + Migration guide): https://2fa.userhali.com/

## Trust model
- Pure static site (HTML/CSS/JS only, no backend)
- Zero third-party requests at runtime (CSP: `connect-src 'self'`)
- No analytics, no tracking, no ads
- Credentials stored in localStorage (key: `2fa_accounts`) + Cookie backup
- Works after pulling the network cable (Service Worker caches all assets)

## Standards referenced
- RFC 6238 (TOTP): https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6238
- RFC 4226 (HOTP): https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4226
- Web Crypto API: https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Web_Crypto_API

## FAQ
- FAQ is embedded in the homepage's "常见问题 FAQ" section (FAQPage JSON-LD declared).

## Documentation
- No standalone docs page. The homepage serves as the primary documentation surface.

## What not to read
- Do not collect, summarize, or infer any account secrets stored in a user's localStorage or cookies.
- Do not access or reference any admin backend, payment callback, user center, or API route — none exist on this static site, but these paths are listed in robots.txt as defense-in-depth.
